understanding.goal_understanding: The objective was to win each level by placing every selectable piece onto goal tiles marked with '+' on the semantic grid, with the additional constraint that if there were multiple goal tiles they all had to be occupied simultaneously by selectable pieces for the engine to report WIN, and to progress through all levels within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn controlled exactly one currently selected piece (shown as P), moved in a grid where each cell corresponded to 3 pixels in the reported [x,y] coordinates; MOVE_* attempted to shift that selected piece one cell in the chosen direction but could be blocked by walls/boundaries/objects, and sometimes boundaries could behave unexpectedly. Some adjacent objects (e.g., blocks) were pushable, so moving into them could push them along if space allowed. Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces (other ones shown as S), and control could be switched between them using CLICK with an exact target_position matching the piece’s pixel/grid coordinates from the state/object lists (not semantic grid indices).
